items.title: Myanmar’s parliament elects coup-leading general as civilian president
items.summary: Myanmar's pro-army parliament on Friday elected Senior General Min Aung Hlaing as president days after he relinquished his top military post, as required by the constitution, to the country's former spymaster. Min Aung Hlaing, the leader of the 2021 coup, was elected after an electoral process dismissed as civilian window dressing by monitoring groups.

items.title: Paris police ban annual French Muslim event over ‘major terrorist risk’ from far-right groups
items.summary: The Annual Gathering of the Muslims of France, which was supposed to run from Friday to Monday, has been banned due to a "major terrorist risk" that it might be targeted by "small far-right groups", Paris police said. The ban order noted that the event was "taking place in a particularly tense international and national context".

items.title: ‘Disturbing trend’ of US-Israeli strikes hitting non-military targets in Iran
items.summary: Reporting from Tehran early Friday, FRANCE 24’s Reza Sayah says there’s been a “disturbing trend” in recent days of US-Israeli strikes hitting non-military targets across Iran. A strike on Iran’s tallest bridge last night killed eight people and wounded 90 others, said Sayah. The war has seen US-Israeli strikes target Iran's civilian airports, fuel depots, hospitals, sports facilities, pharmaceutical manufacturing companies, universities and schools, he notes.

items.title: New Tottenham boss De Zerbi apologises to fans for Greenwood comments
items.summary: De Zerbi told the Spurs website: "I have never wanted to downplay the issue of violence against women, or violence against anyone more broadly."

items.title: Middle East war live: Iran warns UN Security Council against ‘provocative action’ on Hormuz
items.summary: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi on Friday warned against any "provocative action" ahead of an expected UN Security Council vote on the use of force to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Backed by the US, the measure comes amid rising tensions in the region and concerns over global energy supplies. Follow our liveblog for the latest developments.

items.title: Artemis II astronauts ignite engines and head for lunar flyby
items.summary: NASA’s Artemis II astronauts fired their engines and set course for a lunar flyby on Thursday, marking the first human departure from Earth orbit since 1972. The crew of three Americans and one Canadian will break distance records, witness a solar eclipse and pave the way for future lunar missions despite early challenges in orbit.

items.title: Trump signs order threatening up to 100% tariffs on pharmaceuticals
items.summary: US President Donald Trump unveiled sweeping new pharmaceutical tariffs on Thursday that could reach 100 percent unless drugmakers strike pricing deals with his administration. The move escalates his trade strategy and could reshape global supply chains and raise costs.

items.title: Trump sacks attorney general, replaces with ex-personal lawyer
items.summary: US President Donald Trump fired Attorney General Pam Bondi on Thursday and named his former personal lawyer to serve as the acting chief of the Justice Department. The move, which the Republican president announced in a social media post, comes amid criticism of Bondi's handling of the Epstein files and her failure to successfully prosecute several perceived Trump political foes. FRANCE 24's Monte Francis reports.

items.title: 'Any weapon Europe buys from the US is a liability', rearmament expert says
items.summary: European NATO countries have reduced their reliance on the US for weapons imports while significantly increasing their defense spending in the past year. Speaking with FRANCE 24's Sharon Gaffney, Guntram Wolff, Senior Fellow at Bruegel, says that "any weapon we buy as Europeans from the United States is a liability both in terms of technology dependence but also in terms of missing growth and domestic ecosystem that would develop".

items.title: One year on, what remains of Trump's tariffs?
items.summary: It's been a year since US President Donald Trump unveiled sweeping new tariffs, which would change the global trade landscape. But after a year of upheaval and uncertainty, what remains of the 'Liberation Day' duties? Also in the show - soaring oil prices lead to a jump in car-pooling services in France.

items.title: En Birmanie, le chef de la junte Min Aung Hlaing devient président
items.summary: Le chef de la junte birmane, Min Aung Hlaing, a été élu président vendredi par le Parlement. Après avoir pris le pouvoir par la force il y a cinq ans, il pourra continuer à diriger la Birmanie.

translation.title: In Burma, the head of Junta Min Aung Hlaing becomes president
translation.summary: The head of the Burmese junta, Min Aung Hlaing, was elected President on Friday by Parliament. After taking power five years ago, he will be able to continue leading Burma.

items.title: Lyon se qualifie pour le dernier carré de la Ligue des championnes
items.summary: Les Lyonnaises ont renversé Wolfsburg en inscrivant trois buts en prolongation (4-0). Elles affronteront Arsenal en demi-finale de Ligue des championnes.

translation.title: Lyon qualifies for the last square of the Champions League
translation.summary: Les Lyonnaises overturned Wolfsburg by scoring three overtime goals (4-0). They will face Arsenal in the semifinal of the Champions League.

items.title: La justice française refuse de remettre à la Tunisie Halima Ben Ali, cadette de l'ancien autocrate
items.summary: La France ne remettra pas à la Tunisie Halima Ben Ali, selon une décision rendue mercredi par la cour d'appel de Paris. Lors des débats sur la demande d'extradition formulée par Tunis, l'avocate de la fille de l'ancien autocrate avait assuré que renvoyer sa cliente dans son pays reviendrait à une "condamnation à mort".

translation.title: French justice refuses to hand over to Tunisia Halima Ben Ali, younger of the former autocrat
translation.summary: France will not hand over Halima Ben Ali to Tunisia, according to a decision rendered on Wednesday by the Paris Court of Appeal. During the discussions on Tunis's extradition request, the lawyer for the daughter of the former autocrat assured that sending her client back to her country would amount to a "death sentence".

items.title: En direct : l'Iran met en garde le Conseil de sécurité de l'ONU contre toute "action provocatrice"
items.summary: Le ministre iranien des Affaires étrangères Abbas Araghchi a mis en garde vendredi contre toute "action provocatrice" avant un vote attendu du Conseil de sécurité de l'ONU sur un usage de la force pour débloquer le détroit d'Ormuz. Par ailleurs, le président américain Donald Trump a menacé de frapper et de détruire des ponts et des centrales électriques en Iran, quelques heures après la destruction d'un pont près de Téhéran. Suivez notre direct.

translation.title: Live: Iran warns UN Security Council against "provocative action"
translation.summary: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi warned on Friday against any "provocative action" before an expected UN Security Council vote on the use of force to unblock the Strait of Ormuz. In addition, US President Donald Trump threatened to strike and destroy bridges and power plants in Iran a few hours after the destruction of a bridge near Tehran. Follow our direct.

items.title: Donald Trump peut-il claquer la porte de l'Otan ?
items.summary: Mercredi, Donald Trump s’en est pris aux autres Etats membres de l’Otan, au point d’assurer à plusieurs médias qu’il envisageait désormais « absolument » de s’en retirer. La menace n’est pas nouvelle mais le ton est particulièrement véhément qualifiant de « lâches » ses alliés européens, en raison de leurs réticences à venir débloquer le détroit d’Ormuz sous blocus iranien. Finalement, un peu plus tard, lors de son allocution, le président n’a fait aucune allusion à l'Alliance atlantique.

translation.title: Can Donald Trump slam NATO's door?
translation.summary: On Wednesday, Donald Trump was caught up in other NATO member states, to the point of ensuring that several media that he was now "absolutely" considering withdrawing from it. The threat is not new, but the tone is particularly vehemently calling its European allies "loose" because of their reluctance to come and unlock the Strait of Ormuz under Iranian blockade. Finally, a little later, in his speech, the President made no mention of the Atlantic Alliance.

items.title: Présidentielle au Bénin : l'opposition divisée à l'approche du scrutin du 12 avril
items.summary: Au Bénin, le principal parti d'opposition est en pleine déconfiture à quelques jours de la présidentielle. Le parti "les Démocrates" a connu une série de défections au profit du parti au pouvoir. Au Sénégal, c'est l'heure du bilan deux ans après l'investiture de Bassirou Diomaye Faye à la tête du pays. Et en Mauritanie, le gouvernement prend des mesures pour limiter les conséquences de la guerre au Moyen-Orient sur ses citoyens.

translation.title: President in Benin: the divided opposition as the April 12 election approaches
translation.summary: In Benin, the main opposition party is in full disrepair just a few days before the presidential elections. The "Democrats" party experienced a series of defections in favour of the ruling party. In Senegal, it is time for the balance sheet two years after the inauguration of Bassirou Diomaye Faye as head of the country. And in Mauritania, the government is taking steps to limit the consequences of the war in the Middle East on its citizens.

items.title: Ukraine : la Russie n'a pas gagné de territoire en mars, une première depuis deux ans et demi
items.summary: L’armée russe ralentit sa progression en Ukraine depuis fin 2025 et n'a même enregistré aucun gain territorial en mars, selon une analyse de l'AFP des données de l'Institut pour l'étude le la guerre. L'organisme attribue ce ralentissement aux contre-offensives ukrainiennes, mais aussi à l’interdiction faite à la Russie d’utiliser les terminaux Starlink.

translation.title: Ukraine: Russia won no territory in March, first in two and a half years
translation.summary: The Russian army has slowed its progress in Ukraine since the end of 2025 and has not even recorded any territorial gains in March, according to an analysis by the AFP of the data of the Institute for the Study of War. The organization attributes this slowdown to Ukrainian counter-offensives, but also to Russia's ban on using Starlink terminals.

items.title: Le Maire de Saint-Denis, Bally Bagayoko, visé sur CNews : enquête ouverte pour injure raciste
items.summary: Deux enquêtes ont été ouvertes après les propos visant le maire LFI de Saint-Denis, Bally Bagayoko, tenus fin mars sur CNews, a-t-on appris jeudi auprès du parquet de Paris : l'une pour "injure publique" à caractère raciste et l'autre pour cyberharcèlement sur le réseau social X "en raison de sa couleur de peau".

translation.title: The Mayor of Saint-Denis, Bally Bagayoko, targeted on CNews: investigation opened for racist insult
translation.summary: Two investigations were opened after the words against Mayor LFI of Saint-Denis, Bally Bagayoko, held at the end of March on CNews, were learned on Thursday at the Paris Public Prosecutor's Office: one for "public insult" of a racist nature and the other for cyberharassment on social network X "because of its skin color".

items.title: Donald Trump impose des droits de douane à certains médicaments et revoit ceux sur les métaux
items.summary: Le président américain Donald Trump a signé jeudi deux décrets. Le premier impose de nouveaux droits de douane, jusqu'à 100 %, dans le secteur des médicaments, afin d'accélérer la réimplantation d'usines aux États-Unis. Le second remet à plat les droits de douane sur les métaux face aux entreprises qui cherchent à "manipuler artificiellement" les prix de leur production afin de les revendre moins cher aux États-Unis.

translation.title: Donald Trump imposes tariffs on certain drugs and reviews those on metals
translation.summary: US President Donald Trump signed two decrees on Thursday. The former imposes new tariffs, up to 100%, in the pharmaceutical sector, to accelerate the re-establishment of plants in the United States. The second sets tariffs on metals against companies seeking to "manipulate artificially" their production prices in order to sell them cheaper in the United States.

items.title: "Le but de la guerre en Iran n'était pas du tout clair pour Donald Trump" (Eva Joly)
items.summary: Chaque semaine, Judith Grimaldi reçoit un invité dans son podcast "Avec Judith". Cette fois-ci, elle accueille Eva Joly, magistrate et femme politique. Elle a souhaité réagir à l'actualité de la guerre au Moyen-Orient, et plus largement, aux décisions prises par le président américain Donald Trump. Il ne "fait confiance qu'à son instinct", estime-t-elle.Retrouvez l'émission dans son intégralité ce samedi midi sur le site de France 24.

translation.title: "The goal of the war in Iran was not clear to Donald Trump" (Eva Joly)
translation.summary: Every week, Judith Grimaldi receives a guest in her podcast "With Judith". This time, she welcomes Eva Joly, judge and politician. She wanted to respond to the current situation of the Middle East war, and more broadly to the decisions taken by US President Donald Trump. He "trusts only his instincts," she says. See the entire show on Saturday noon on the website of France 24.

items.title: Silicon Valley Bank collapse renews calls to address disparities impacting entrepreneurs of color
items.summary: When customers at Silicon Valley Bank rushed to withdraw billions of dollars last month, venture capitalist Arlan Hamilton stepped in to help some of the founders of color who panicked about losing access to payroll funds.

items.title: Not only is Lake Powell's water level plummeting because of drought, its total capacity is shrinking, too
items.summary: Lake Powell, the second-largest human-made reservoir in the US, has lost nearly 7% of its potential storage capacity since 1963, when Glen Canyon Dam was built, a new report shows.

items.title: These were the best and worst places for air quality in 2021, new report shows
items.summary: Air pollution spiked to unhealthy levels around the world in 2021, according to a new report.

items.title: Big-box stores could help slash emissions and save millions by putting solar panels on roofs. Why aren't more of them doing it?
items.summary: As the US attempts to wean itself off its heavy reliance on fossil fuels and shift to cleaner energy sources, many experts are eyeing a promising solution: your neighborhood big-box stores and shopping malls.

items.title: Look of the Week: Blackpink headline Coachella in Korean hanboks
items.summary: Bringing the second day of this year's Coachella to a close, K-Pop girl group Blackpink made history Saturday night when they became the first Asian act to ever headline the festival. To a crowd of, reportedly, over 125,000 people, Jennie, Jisoo, Lisa and Rosé used the ground-breaking moment to pay homage to Korean heritage by arriving onstage in hanboks: a traditional type of dress.

items.title: Scientists identify secret ingredient in Leonardo da Vinci paintings
items.summary: "Old Masters" such as Leonardo da Vinci, Sandro Botticelli and Rembrandt may have used proteins, especially egg yolk, in their oil paintings, according to a new study.

items.title: Iran's tallest bridge collapses after reported US airstrikes; Iran threatens American allies in retaliation
items.summary: Iran's biggest bridge near Tehran crashes down after reported U.S. airstrikes as Trump presses the regime to make a deal before escalation.

items.title: Jewish communities on high alert as Passover begins amid rising security threats nationwide
items.summary: Jewish communities face a heightened security threat this Passover, with NYPD Commissioner Jessica Tisch warning of an elevated alert as antisemitic attacks rise across the U.S.

items.title: Nepal probe exposes $20M fraudulent rescue scheme involving lacing Mt Everest hikers' food: report
items.summary: Insurance companies are being scammed out of money by fraudulent rescue schemes in the nation of Nepal, according to a report by The Kathmandu Post.

items.title: Iran regime uses war to mask 'brutal' execution surge against political opponents
items.summary: A State Department spokesperson called Iran's executions "barbaric" as the United Nations and activists group report the regime is using the death penalty to crush dissent.

items.title: Israeli paramedic delivers baby, rushes it to bomb shelter during Iran attack
items.summary: Israeli paramedic Dr. Gal Rosen delivered a baby in Tel Aviv, then raced to a bomb shelter with the newborn as Iron Dome missile sirens blared overhead.

items.title: Ukraine signals progress on US security guarantees after call with Trump envoys
items.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy says talks with Trump envoys Witkoff and Kushner showed progress on Ukraine security guarantees, calling for a leaders' summit to advance peace.

items.title: Nigeria's Christians on edge for Easter after Palm Sunday massacre
items.summary: Nigeria, ranked seventh-worst in the world for Christian persecution, saw a reported 28 killed on Palm Sunday, with fears Christians could be targeted this Easter.

items.title: Iran's ceasefire push may be a 'cycle of deception,' analysts warn as shadowy figure gains power
items.summary: Iran's shadowy Ahmad Vahidi, a former Quds Force commander linked to deadly bombings abroad, is now more dominant than Iran's president or Khamenei's son, experts warn.

items.title: Turkey’s NATO role under scrutiny amid new report on Hamas, Muslim Brotherhood ties
items.summary: A new Foundation for Defense of Democracies report alleges Turkey under Erdogan has shifted toward Islamist movements, raising NATO alliance concerns ahead of a July summit in Ankara, Turkey.

items.title: Israel approves sweeping death penalty legislation targeting terrorism, EU condemns move
items.summary: Israel's Knesset voted to mandate the death penalty for terrorists convicted of deadly attacks, a shift lawmakers say was driven by the horrors of the Oct. 7 massacre.

items.title: Pakistan says a new round of peace talks with Afghanistan is underway in China
items.summary: Pakistan confirmed it was holding peace talks with Afghanistan's Taliban government in China, where Beijing is trying to broker a lasting ceasefire after weeks of fighting.

items.title: Cuba releasing 2,010 prisoners as the US pressures the island's government
items.summary: The Cuban government said the pardons were a "humanitarian gesture" in connection with Holy Week and didn't mention mounting pressures with the U.S.

items.title: U.K. convenes 40 nations to discuss Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Foreign ministers from more than 40 countries are meeting virtually to discuss President Trump's request for help in ensuring shipping in the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Trump makes case for Iran war. And, SCOTUS leans toward upholding birthright citizenship
items.summary: President Trump addressed the nation last night, making his case for war with Iran. And, the Supreme Court majority seemed inclined to rule against the Trump administration on birthright citizenship.

items.title: Hegseth ousts the Army chief of staff as the Iran war rages through Week 5
items.summary: U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth asked Army Chief of Staff Randy George to step down and retire, a U.S. official confirmed to NPR.

items.title: Pakistan is playing intermediary in the Iran war, a role it has played before
items.summary: Pakistan has emerged as a key intermediary in the U.S-Israel war with Iran. It played this role before, during a high-stakes moment in diplomatic history.

items.title: U.S. lifts sanctions on Venezuela's acting President Delcy Rodríguez
items.summary: The newly announced sanctions relief is the latest U.S. recognition of Rodríguez as a legitimate authority in Venezuela ever since the U.S. military captured her predecessor, Nicolás Maduro.

items.title: 25 years ago today, a mid-air collision sparked an 11-day U.S.–China standoff
items.summary: On April 1, 2001, a midair collision between an American aircraft and a Chinese plane sparked an 11-day diplomatic crisis — and the two countries still tell very different stories.

items.title: Who is held accountable if a war crime is committed in Iran?
items.summary: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with international law expert Gabor Rona about attacks on civilian infrastructure in the Iran conflict and how they may constitute war crimes.

items.summary: The border town of Van in Turkey is known for being a hub for Iranian travelers - and its cats.

The Circular stressed that the Fourth National Agricultural Census was a major national survey on the new quest for a comprehensive revival of the Chinese nation through China's modernization, guided by the Chinese socialist ideology of Xi Jinping's new era, and guided by the spirit of the 20th and 20th China-China Congresses, and by the serious implementation of the Party's central and State Council's decision-making process. It was determined that the census should be conducted in accordance with the law, that the scientific census should be conducted and that the census should be conducted for the people's census, that it should be carried out in accordance with the law, that it should be carried out in a realistic and innovative manner, and that the “three farmers” of the new era should be thoroughly examined in order to objectively reflect new developments in agricultural development, new aspects of rural construction, new changes in rural life and new effects of rural reform. The Circular states that the Fourth National Agricultural Survey is aimed at rural households in the People ' s Republic of China, including rural agricultural producers and other households; urban agricultural producers; agricultural production units; villagers ' committees; and town and town people ' s governments. The main elements of the census include agricultural production conditions, food and food production, new quality of agricultural productivity, basics of rural development, living conditions of rural residents, etc. The standard time of the census is 2400 hours on 31 December 2026, and the data for the period is annual 2026. items.summary: 新华社北京6月5日电 中央军委主席习近平日前签署命令，发布《军事设施建设条例》，自2025年8月1日起施行。 条例深入贯彻习近平强军思想，深入贯彻新时代军事战略方针，聚焦服务备战打仗，适应新的领导指挥体制和保障模式，科学规范军事设施建设的基本原则、管理体制、运行机制和工作制度，是军事设施建设工作的重要依据。 条例共11章63条，按照军委管总、战区主战、军种主建的总原则，围绕理顺建设链、管理链、监督链，重点从军事设施建设计划管理、项目审批、工程发包、勘察设计、施工管理、应急建设、安全保密、监督问责等方面，厘清管理权限，优化流程机制，固化实践成果，为提高军事设施建设质量效益和保障能力提供有力法治保障。

items.title: 全国人大常委会启动节约能源法执法检查
items.summary: 新华社北京6月5日电 为推动节约能源法全面有效实施，促进更高水平、更高质量做好节能工作，全国人大常委会5日启动节约能源法执法检查。 节约能源法自1998年1月1日实施，并于2007年、2016年、2018年进行了修改。 记者5日从全国人大常委会节约能源法执法检查组第一次全体会议上了解到，为全面深入了解法律实施情况，此次执法检查采取实地检查与委托检查相结合的方式，检查组将赴天津、江苏、湖南、广东、广西、四川等省（区、市）开展实地检查，并委托北京、河北、山西、吉林、上海、重庆等省（市）人大常委会对本行政区域内节约能源法实施情况进行检查。检查组将采取实地检查、听取汇报、组织座谈、典型调研、专题研究、抽查暗访等多种方式，全面准确了解法律实施情况，对带有普遍性的问题深入调查研究。 检查组将重点检查8方面内容：落实节能管理制度情况，推进重点领域节能工作情况，重点用能单位节能管理情况，推动节能技术进步情况，实施节能激励措施情况，与法律配套的行政法规、部门规章以及地方性法规的制定情况，法律实施中存在的其他问题，对节约能源法修改的意见和建议。

translation.title: The Standing Committee of the National People ' s Congress initiated an energy-saving law enforcement inspection
translation.summary: In order to promote the full and effective implementation of the Energy Conservation Act and to promote higher and higher-quality energy efficiency, the Standing Committee of the National People ' s Congress launched an energy conservation law enforcement inspection on 5 June. The Energy Conservation Act has been in force since 1 January 1998 and was amended in 2007, 2016 and 2018. On 5 May, journalists learned from the first plenary meeting of the Standing Committee of the National People ' s Congress ' s Standing Committee on Energy Conservation Law Enforcement Inspection Unit that, in order to obtain a comprehensive and in-depth understanding of the implementation of the law, the inspection took the form of a combination of on-site inspections and commissioned inspections, which will be carried out in the provinces (districts and municipalities) of Tianjin, Jiangsu, Hunan, Guangdong, Guangxi, Sichuan and Beijing, Hebei, Shanxi, Jilin, Shanghai and Chongqing, among others. The Unit will have a comprehensive and accurate understanding of the implementation of the law and will conduct in-depth research and studies on issues of a general nature, in a variety of ways, including on-site inspections, debriefings, organization of talks, typical research, thematic studies, spot-checking and unannounced visits. The Unit will focus on eight areas: implementation of energy-saving management systems; promotion of energy-saving efforts in priority areas; focus on energy-saving management in energy-use units; promotion of technological progress in energy-saving; implementation of energy-saving incentives; development of administrative, sectoral and local regulations in conjunction with the law; other issues in the implementation of the law; and suggestions and recommendations for changes to the energy-saving law.

The incident highlights ongoing challenges despite a recent US waiver.

items.title: Bridge for bridge: Iran lists 7 Gulf targets after Karaj strike
items.summary: Following US-led strikes on a key bridge near Tehran, Iran is signaling potential retaliation across the Gulf. The attack, confirmed by President Trump, destroyed Iran's tallest bridge, prompting Tehran to consider a "tit for tat" response. Iranian media have identified strategic bridges in the region as possible targets, indicating a widening conflict.

items.title: ‘My issue with Sanju was…’ Yuvraj Singh on flaw that had Samson struggling
items.summary: Yuvraj Singh shared how his advice on footwork helped Sanju Samson overcome a career slump. Samson's improved technique was evident in the 2026 T20 World Cup. He became India's top scorer and Player of the Tournament. Samson's final innings set a new record for the highest individual score in a T20 World Cup final.

items.title: Google CEO Sundar Pichai to 'return' to Stanford University in June 2026
items.summary: Google and Alphabet CEO Sundar Pichai will deliver Stanford University's 2026 Commencement address. A Stanford alumnus, Pichai expressed his privilege to address the graduating class. This marks his first in-person commencement speech, following a virtual address to the Class of 2020 during the pandemic, where he encouraged resilience and embracing change.

items.title: AAP rift out in open: Raghav Chadha attacks leadership; party hits back
items.summary: Raghav Chadha, removed as AAP's Rajya Sabha deputy leader, asserted his silence in Parliament is not defeat. He questioned the party's high command after AAP requested the Rajya Sabha Secretariat to stop him from speaking, stating his focus has always been on raising public issues.

items.title: Yuvraj on SRH’s Abhishek captaincy snub: ‘Now with what frame of mind will he play’?
items.summary: Former India cricketer Yuvraj Singh, Abhishek Sharma's mentor, has stated that it is disappointing for any player who has given his ‘heart and soul’ to the franchise ‘ for years’ to have missed out on leading the side. SRH had announced Ishan Kishan as the stand-in skipper for IPL 2026, snubbing Abhishek and the decision had not gone down well with a section of the fans and even some cricket experts.

items.title: US imposes 100% tariff on patented pharma imports: How it impacts India
items.summary: The US has imposed up to 100% tariffs on patented drugs and certain metals, aiming to boost domestic manufacturing. While India's generic drug exports are largely exempt, firms producing branded medicines or inputs for patented drugs may face pressure. Developed nations are expected to bear the brunt of these new measures.

items.title: Iran claims IRGC Navy struck Oracle facility in Dubai, escalating tech-targeted attacks
items.summary: Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) claims it struck an Oracle data center in Dubai, following threats against US tech firms. This follows a reported attack on an Amazon cloud center in Bahrain. The IRGC warned employees of 18 US companies, including Microsoft and Google, to evacuate facilities, citing retaliation for attacks in Iran.

items.title: Guerra no Irã desloca milhões e ameaça agravar crises humanitárias além do Oriente Médio; veja gráficos
items.summary:
Mais de 4,5 milhões de pessoas deixaram suas casas no último mês para tentar escapar de ataques da guerra no Oriente Médio. Contando Líbano e Irã, são ao menos 3.235 mortos e 28.735 feridos. O conflito iniciado por Estados Unidos e Israel suscita uma crise humanitária que, em pouco mais de 30 dias, se agrava com a crise econômica causada por anos de sanções ocidentais.
Leia mais (04/02/2026 - 23h00)

translation.title: Iran's war shifts millions and threatens to aggravate humanitarian crises beyond the Middle East; see graphs
translation.summary:
Over 4.5 million people have left their homes in the last month to try to escape war attacks in the Middle East. Counting Lebanon and Iran, they are at least 3,235 dead and 28,735 injured. The conflict initiated by the United States and Israel raises a humanitarian crisis that, in just over 30 days, worsens with the economic crisis caused by years of Western sanctions.

translation.title: Cuba announces release of 2,010 prisoners

items.title: Em Tóquio, Macron fala em missão para reabrir estreito de Hormuz ao fim da guerra
items.summary:

translation.title: In Tokyo, Macron speaks of mission to reopen Hormuz Strait at the end of the war
translation.summary:

The announcement was made on Wednesday (1o) by the Japanese Prime Minister, Sanae Takaichi.
